AUTHORITATIVE SPEC: /home/polaris/polaris_project/GATE_GENERALIZE_FIX45_PLAN.md (the 3-way Fable+Kimi+Codex consolidation). Read the relevant section IN FULL before editing. Do NOT improvise beyond it.

HARD GUARDRAILS (a violation fails the run):
- EDIT ONLY /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent, branch gate-inversion, building on the committed HEAD d44ee36. NEVER touch /home/polaris/wt/flywheel.
- FAITHFULNESS FROZEN: NEVER modify src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py (incl frozen banner builder build_d8_unadjudicated_banner ~:3212 and evidence-support ~:3124), strict_verify, NLI, D8 thresholds, or the drop rule. No new verification pass, no new faithfulness test. Verify 0-diff at the end vs both d44ee36's provenance_generator.py and champion df4118a.
- EVIDENCE-POSITIVE ONLY, ABSOLUTE: Fix 5 may ONLY move a source UNKNOWN->PASS (via validated tier) or reorder/prefer it. It must NEVER turn any PASS into FAIL, and NEVER relax an existing FAIL. (The plan REJECTED Kimi's FAIL-relaxation guard — do not implement it.) Retraction / predatory-host / explicit is_peer_reviewed=False FAILs stay absolute and first.
- DOI-ALONE IS NOT T1: a bare DOI (preprint/dataset/predatory/content-shell) must NOT auto-PASS. T1 requires validated peer-reviewed OR official-gov/primary evidence. journal=>PASS is ONE T1 instance, not a special case.
- abacademies.org: it is a NEW predatory FAIL, NOT evidence-positive. Add it (if at all) as a clearly-labelled separate denylist line, NOT inside the UNKNOWN->PASS resolver, so the "FAIL behavior untouched" claim stays true.
- RETIRE journal_only_filter.py (filter_to_citeable / assert_no_leak / JournalOnlyAbort + its ~10 call-sites) — it is a hard fail-closed frozen-corpus MASK (violates "enforce scope at RETRIEVAL, never filter a frozen corpus", which tanked RACE 0.4447->0.3264). Replace with the adequacy+acquisition-receipt-gated PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_HARD eligibility path per the plan. If any call-site is load-bearing, neutralize to a no-op behind the gate, do not leave a live mask.
- HARD source-kind enforcement arms ONLY behind corpus-adequacy (>= min in-scope-kind rows) AND a matching acquisition receipt (contract_hash). Otherwise degrade to prefer + a disclosure line in the audit appendix. Adequacy counts in-scope-KIND rows, never DOI/journal rows. Exclusions always win and are never adequacy-checked.
- NO journal/review/"Introduction and Scope" LITERALS in control-flow. They live only as DATA in the archetype/tier registries. (An anti-hardcode grep test enforces this.)
- Methods is NOT always machinery: keep it in the body when contract.sections requires it; only internal audit/disclosure/reliability/ledger blocks are demoted to the appendix.
- Do NOT rebuild predicate_force["allowed_source_kinds"] (retrieval_projection.py:695) or the op="prefer" projection (:336) — already general per the plan.
- PG_GATE-OFF and all-flags-default MUST be byte-identical to champion (golden-hash test). New behavior behind default-OFF/None flags, double-guarded by contract-presence.
- REJECTED HAZARDS (do none): PG_REPORT_D8_BANNER=0; editing the frozen banner builder; institution-allowlist hard PASS; prose-rewrite/re-stitch of verified Key-Findings spans; editing vendored clean_article.py; generative/LLM report skeleton; LLM inside score_source_quality.
- Offline only — NO live retrieval/compose (10-min cap). Verify anchors against the live committed code before editing (line numbers may have drifted).

Implement the GENERALIZED Fix 5 (source-kind + credibility) per /home/polaris/polaris_project/GATE_GENERALIZE_FIX45_PLAN.md §3/§4, on top of Fix 4's tree. Do exactly:
1. RETIRE journal_only_filter.py: remove/neutralize filter_to_citeable / assert_no_leak / JournalOnlyAbort and ALL call-sites (the plan lists them). It is the frozen-corpus hard mask that must die. Replace with the eligibility path below. Leave NO live mask; if a call-site is structural, make it a gated no-op.
2. Replace the journal "4.5 second-chance" (_has_doi_or_journal_credential) in score_source_quality with a deterministic signal->(tier,kind) REGISTRY consulted BEFORE the UNKNOWN fail-closed: T1 = validated peer-reviewed journal metadata OR official gov/primary/statute/authenticated-filing -> PASS unconditionally; T2 = reputable newswire/authenticated IR/established analyst w/ author+method -> PASS iff kind in allowed and not excluded; T3/unrated -> stay UNKNOWN/demote. DOI-ALONE is NOT T1. First match wins with a recorded basis; no match -> existing UNKNOWN path unchanged. score_source_quality gains policy=None => byte-identical default. NO LLM in the verdict fn (PG_CREDIBILITY_LLM_TIERING only fills metadata upstream).
3. Kind eligibility + adequacy: add build_source_kind_eligibility(policy, rows, acquisition_receipt): exclusions (NOT_IN) always win first (monotonic union of excluded ids; a PASS/weight can never re-include an excluded kind). Hard allowed_source_kinds arms a nonmatching-mask ONLY if the acquisition receipt.contract_hash matches AND distinct in-scope-kind usable rows >= PG_SOURCE_KIND_MIN_ADEQUATE (default 25); else downgrade hard->soft (prefer), record an Assumption + SourceReceipt basis, and disclose in the audit appendix. Count in-scope-KIND rows, never DOI/journal.
4. Selection ordering: replace _is_journal_lead with _kind_match(row in allowed_source_kinds) stable sort (kind-match desc, quality weight desc, existing rank); empty allowed -> no reorder (byte-identical). S4 audit metric = cited in-scope-kind share (NOT journal share); empty scope -> silent.
5. Topicality: generalize build_topicality_eligibility with optional soft_floor (score<hard 0.15 quarantine; 0.15<=score<0.30 soft-demote even when hard; >=0.30 unchanged); soft_floor=None => byte-identical. Keep fail-OPEN when score_map is None.
6. Rename PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_JOURNAL_ONLY -> PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_HARD (kind-agnostic). abacademies.org, if added, is a clearly-commented SEPARATE predatory-denylist line (a new FAIL), NOT part of the evidence-positive resolver.
All UPSTREAM of the frozen verifier; provenance_generator.py 0-diff. Report edits (file:line).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# GATE — Generalize Fix 4 (report shape) + Fix 5 (source-kind/quality eligibility)
2	
3	> **This supersedes the Fable-only v1.** v1 was one external design (Fable) adjudicated by OPUS against a *baseline* (`78fe2ca`) whose line numbers no longer match reality. This v2 is a **genuine 3-way review** (Fable 5, Kimi K3, Codex Sol all returned) cross-checked against the **actually-committed** journal-flavored build at **`d44ee36`** on branch `gate-inversion` (worktree `/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent`). All edit anchors below are verified against `d44ee36`.
4	
5	**Author:** OPUS (3-way consolidation)
6	**Date:** 2026-07-17
7	**Landed build under review:** `d44ee36` — "gate(inversion): apply the consolidated 5-fix plan …"
8	**Reviewers who returned:** FABLE 5, KIMI K3, CODEX SOL (all three)
9	
10	---
11	
12	## 0. Governing constraints (frozen)
13	
14	- **C1 FAITHFULNESS FROZEN.** `provenance_generator.py`, strict_verify, drop rule, NLI, D8: **0-diff**. No new verification pass, no new faithfulness test.
15	- **C2 SCOPE AT RETRIEVAL, never hard-filter a frozen corpus** (997→131 tanked RACE 0.4447→0.3264, IF fell too). Hard strength buys *go-find + reorder + audit*, not a mask — unless the corpus is *provably adequate*.
16	- **C3 MINIMAL / anti-over-engineering.** Smallest faithfulness-safe change. This run's job is *prove-not-a-regression* (≈ champion IF 0.4587), not beat-champion. One env flag per fix, default OFF/prior; champion path double-guarded.
17	- **C4 Coverage > Insight > Readability > IF.**
18	
19	### Reality of the landed build (this is what we generalize ON TOP OF)
20	
21	`d44ee36` shipped Fix 4/5 in **journal-literal** form:
22	
23	| Surface | Landed at `d44ee36` | File:anchor |
24	|---|---|---|
25	| Fix 4 shape | `PG_REPORT_LITREVIEW_SHAPE` **default ON**; hardcoded `## Introduction and Scope`; fixed order sections→depth→**KF→**biblio (KF always *after* thematic) | `run_honest_sweep_r3.py` `build_intro_and_scope_md` :6398, `reshape_report_body_litreview` :6424, call-site :17463 |
26	| Fix 4 table | drb_72-specific summary-table injection | `render_summary_table_into_artifact` :6454, call-site :17802 |
27	| Fix 5b | journal/DOI "second-chance" PASS at step "4.5" | `quality_eligibility.py` `_has_doi_or_journal_credential`, inserted in `score_source_quality` |
28	| Fix 5d | `_is_journal_lead` selection ordering keyed on `"journal" in allowed_source_kinds` | `run_honest_sweep_r3.py` :14609–14636 |
29	| Journal-only **hard mask** | an entire node module `journal_only_filter.py` — fail-closed corpus filter (`filter_to_citeable`, `assert_no_leak`, `prune_contract_plans`, `JournalOnlyAbort`), flag `PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_JOURNAL_ONLY` + protocol `source_restriction: journal_only`; wired at ~10 call-sites (`run_honest_sweep_r3.py` :10819, :12092, :14263, :14843, :15322, …) | `src/polaris_graph/nodes/journal_only_filter.py` |
30	
31	**Already-general in the baseline (do NOT rebuild — all three models were right that the mechanism exists):**
32	- `retrieval_projection.py:695` already sets `predicate_force["allowed_source_kinds"]="hard" if is_hard else "soft"` from the ContractTerm force (clause ledger: restrict/oblige/exclude⇒HARD; prefer/quality/date⇒PREFER).
33	- `to_scope_protocol` (:336) already emits every allowed kind as `op="prefer"`, `strictness="hard"` iff the predicate is hard. **No journal literal there.**
34	- KF bullet integrity `_bullet_marker_integrity_ok` (:877) + `_reemit_key_findings_bullet` (:891) are kind-agnostic; `abacademies.org` is already in `_PREDATORY_HOST_PATTERNS`.
35	
36	> **The single biggest reality-gap vs v1:** v1 assumed Fix 5d was *only* selection ordering and no hard journal flag shipped. In fact `d44ee36` ships a **whole hard fail-closed journal-only corpus filter** (`journal_only_filter.py`) — the exact C2-violating "mask a frozen corpus" pattern all three models warned against. It is **dormant** (`JOURNAL_ONLY_BENCHMARK_SLUGS = frozenset()`, flag default `0`), but it exists and is wired. The generalization must decide its fate (see §5, §7).
37	
38	---
39	
40	## 1. Convergence map (honest 3-way)
41	
42	### Fix 4 — report shape
43	
44	**All THREE agreed (strongest signal):**
45	1. **Closed archetype table, NOT a generative/LLM skeleton.** Fable: "Option 1 … deterministic, unit-testable, OFF-path trivially byte-identical" and Option 2 "walks straight at C1." Kimi: "closed archetype set — pick this, reject generative … a generative (LLM) skeleton … is exactly the 'new subsystem' the operator kills." Codex: "Use a small registry, not an LLM-generated skeleton. A generative skeleton adds nondeterminism, creates opportunities to invent sections or rewrite claims."
46	2. **"review / Introduction-and-Scope" is just ONE row of the table**, keyed off normalized `deliverable.kind`, default `review`, resolved via the *existing* `_resolve_facet_id`-style synonym pattern (no new vocab). All three list ≈ review / memo / brief / comparison / explainer.
47	3. **Arrange existing render blocks only — never edit block payload.** Fable: "pure permutation/concat of opaque block strings." Kimi: "a permutation of concatenation operands." Codex: "handles strings as opaque payloads. Verified sentences remain byte-identical."
48	4. **The framing paragraph is claim-free, citation-free, from the objective spans** — same faithfulness class as the abort-path H1s. All three say identical.
49	5. **The D8 banner is the *actual* fix for "opens on a blockquote"; adding an H1 at the assembly seam does not fix it** because the banner is prepended later (`:20778`). All three independently flag this; all three say do **not** edit the frozen builder (`provenance_generator.py:3212`) — relocate the *insertion*, keep bytes identical. (v1 chose to leave the banner alone and accept the blockquote-above-H1; see §2.4 adjudication.)
50	
51	**Two agreed:**
52	- **Key-Findings position varies by archetype (BLUF for memo/brief, after-framing for review).** Fable and Kimi make this the load-bearing degree of freedom; Fable: "a memo/brief audience wants findings first (BLUF)". Codex encodes it in its per-archetype slot tables (memo = "summary/key findings" early) but does not name a `kf_position` field.
53	- **Methods is NOT unconditionally machinery.** Kimi: "Blanket 'demote methods below the appendix' is wrong for systematic-review contracts, where Methods is scored content (PRISMA) … `contract.sections` already tells you which sections are required." Codex: "User-requested methods and substantive limitations are not automatically machinery." **Fable dissents** (see below).
54	
55	**Single-model dissent:**
56	- **Fable: machinery placement is archetype-INVARIANT** — methods/disclosures/reliability all go below the appendix boundary for *every* archetype ("this is the key simplification"). This directly contradicts Kimi+Codex on Methods. **Adjudication in §7.**
57	- **Codex: split `allowed` (exclusive IN) vs `required` (REQUIRE, non-exclusive) vs `preferred` (PREFER) as three distinct policy lists**, driven by `ContractTerm.operator` *together with* `force`, not force alone. Fable and Kimi collapse to `allowed_source_kinds + predicate_force`. (This is a Fix-5 point but Codex is alone on it — §7.)
58	- **Kimi: archetype also relabels the KF/framing headings** (review `## Key Findings` vs memo `## Bottom Line`). Fable explicitly says keep `## Key Findings` verbatim everywhere in v1; Codex is silent. **Adjudication: keep verbatim (Fable) for v1** — relabeling is chrome churn with no measured payoff.
59	
60	### Fix 5 — source-kind + quality eligibility
61	
62	**All THREE agreed (strongest signal):**
63	1. **The journal-literal parts actively HARM non-journal contracts** (not merely fail to help). Fable: "It actively fights non-journal contracts." Kimi: "will actively harm the five non-journal prompts." Codex: "would mis-handle news, government, press-release, and blog-scoped prompts."
64	2. **UNKNOWN resolves by a general signal→tier model; "journal ⇒ PASS" is ONE T1 instance, not the mechanism.** Fable: signal→(tier,kind) table, "journal ⇒ PASS falls out as the T1-scholarly row." Kimi: deterministic signal registry, "the plan's 5b, demoted to one row." Codex: "journal PASS falls out from 'validated peer-reviewed scholarly source' … not from special-casing the prompt."
65	3. **T1 = authoritative-universal → PASS unconditionally; T2/T3 PASS only iff kind ∈ allowed_source_kinds and not excluded.** Fable states this rule verbatim. Kimi: T1 signals PASS + a "contract-relative FAIL guard" when kind ∈ allowed. Codex: "Allow only validated T1/T2 to convert … UNKNOWN into PASS," peer-reviewed-journal AND gov both T1.
66	4. **Adequacy must count in-scope-kind rows, not DOI/journal rows**, and hard enforcement arms **only** behind adequacy, else prefer + disclose. All three, verbatim. Codex adds an **acquisition-receipt** precondition (below).
67	5. **Exclusion always wins; evidence-positive only (never re-admit a strict_verify FAIL, never down-move a verdict); all Fix-5 code is upstream of the frozen verifier.** All three, verbatim.
68	
69	**Two agreed:**
70	- **Rename/kill `PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_JOURNAL_ONLY`.** Fable: rename → `PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_HARD`, no alias. Kimi: "rename … kind-agnostic (or drop the env entirely and drive from `predicate_force`)." Codex: implicit (no journal branch anywhere). **Two name it; Codex would drive it from policy without a dedicated env.**
71	- **DOI alone is NOT sufficient T1 evidence** — Codex and Kimi both call this out (preprints/datasets/predatory carry DOIs). Codex: "DOI alone is not T1 evidence." Kimi: "DOI-only preprint does not [pass]." **Fable's table treats DOI-or-journal as the T1-scholarly predicate** (reusing the landed `_has_doi_or_journal_credential`, which *does* already reject preprint DOIs and predatory hosts upstream). **Adjudication in §7.**
72	
73	**Single-model dissent:**
74	- **Codex: `abacademies.org` is NOT "evidence-positive only" — it creates a new FAIL**, so treat it as a separately-approved denylist correction, "otherwise the plan's claim that FAIL behavior is untouched is false." Fable/Kimi keep it inline as shipped. **Codex is correct on the letter** (it is already committed at `d44ee36`, so this is moot for the unwind, but note it honestly).
75	- **Codex: require an acquisition receipt** (`{contract_hash, source_policy_applied, retrieved_with_kind_lanes}`) matching the policy hash before a hard allowlist may arm — "A frozen unscoped corpus therefore always degrades to preference plus disclosure." Fable/Kimi gate hard-arming on the adequacy count alone. **Codex's receipt is the stronger C2 guarantee; adopt a lightweight form — §7.**
76	- **Codex: three-way operator split** (allowed/required/preferred). Fable/Kimi: two-way (allowed + exclude, force = strength). **Adjudication: defer required-vs-allowed to a labelled TODO — §7.**
77	- **Kimi: a "contract-relative FAIL guard"** that *relaxes* the `is_peer_reviewed=False`/low-tier FAIL to neutral UNKNOWN when the row's kind ∈ allowed. **Fable and Codex both refuse to touch FAIL branches.** Codex: "Explicit quality FAIL precedence remains before positive UNKNOWN resolution." **Adjudication: REJECT Kimi's FAIL-relaxation (§7)** — it violates "evidence-positive only," the one invariant all three otherwise share; the contradictory contract is a conflict-ledger case, not a silent relaxation.
78	
79	---
80	
81	## 2. FIX 4 — the generalized design (adjudicated)
82	
83	### 2.1 Closed archetype table (table, not generative — 3/3 converge)
84	
85	```python
86	# NEW: src/polaris_graph/generator/report_skeleton.py  (pure; no I/O, no LLM, no provenance import)
87	@dataclass(frozen=True)
88	class Archetype:
89	    key: str            # review | memo | brief | comparison | explainer
90	    framing_title: str  # "Introduction and Scope" | "" | "Executive Summary" | "Scope and Criteria" | "Overview"
91	    kf_position: str    # "lead" (BLUF) | "after_framing" | "tail"
92	
93	ARCHETYPES = {
94	  "review":     Archetype("review",     "Introduction and Scope", "after_framing"),
95	  "memo":       Archetype("memo",       "",                       "lead"),
96	  "brief":      Archetype("brief",      "Executive Summary",      "lead"),
97	  "comparison": Archetype("comparison", "Scope and Criteria",     "after_framing"),
98	  "explainer":  Archetype("explainer",  "Overview",               "tail"),
99	}
100	KIND_SYNONYMS = {  # normalized deliverable.kind value -> archetype key
101	  "literature review":"review","systematic review":"review","survey":"review","review":"review",
102	  "memo":"memo","decision memo":"memo","briefing memo":"memo",
103	  "brief":"brief","policy brief":"brief","executive brief":"brief",
104	  "comparison":"comparison","comparative analysis":"comparison","market scan":"comparison",
105	  "explainer":"explainer","primer":"explainer","overview":"explainer",
106	}
107	DEFAULT_ARCHETYPE = "review"   # least-wrong universal shape; disclosed via Assumption when assumed
108	```
109	
110	Machinery placement is **archetype-invariant** (Fable), *with one Kimi/Codex carve-out*: the machinery set = `{methods, cwf_disclosed, drop_disclosure, reliability}` goes below the existing `_AUDIT_MACHINERY_APPENDIX_BOUNDARY` for **every** archetype — **except** `methods` stays in the scored body when a `SectionRequirement` in `contract.sections` matches it (Kimi/Codex's systematic-review case). This is the *only* adjudicated addition to Fable's invariant-machinery rule; it is a 2-line guard (`methods_is_machinery = not _contract_requires_section(contract, "methods")`), not a new subsystem. See §7.
111	
112	Only two things vary per archetype: **KF position** and **framing title**. Ordered skeleton:
113	
114	```
115	# {question}                        (H1 — existing "# Research report: {q}")
116	[KF]          if kf_position == "lead"          (memo / brief — BLUF)
117	[framing]     if framing_title != ""            (## {framing_title} from objective)
118	[KF]          if kf_position == "after_framing" (review / comparison)
119	sections_concat                                 (thematic ### — NEVER reordered internally)
120	_depth_layer                                    (synthesis)
121	[KF]          if kf_position == "tail"          (explainer — keep heading "## Key Findings")
122	biblio_section
123	## Appendix: audit, disclosure, and weighting (not scored as report claims)   (existing boundary, verbatim)
124	methods* + cwf_disclosed + drop_disclosure      (* methods here iff not a required section)
125	[reliability appended UNDER the same boundary]
126	```
127	
128	Three pure functions: `resolve_archetype(contract) -> (Archetype, assumed:bool)`, `build_framing_md(contract, archetype) -> str`, `order_report_blocks(archetype, blocks, *, methods_is_machinery) -> (scored_body, machinery_appendix)`.
129	
130	### 2.2 The drb_72 summary table (`render_summary_table_into_artifact`)
131	
132	This is genuinely prompt-driven ("some prompts explicitly ask the report to END with a titled summary TABLE and name the exact column headers"). It already reads `contract_headers` from the contract — so it is **already contract-driven**, not journal-hardcoded. **Keep it, but gate its invocation on a contract term** (`deliverable.format == "table"` / presence of `contract_headers`), not a slug. Verify at implementation that the call-site (:17802) is not slug-gated; if it is, replace the slug check with the contract-term check. This is the one Fix-4 surface none of the three models saw (it is not in the pack); flag it and generalize its *trigger*, not its body.
133	
134	### 2.3 Exact edits (Fix 4) ON TOP OF `d44ee36`
135	
136	1. **NEW `src/polaris_graph/generator/report_skeleton.py`** — the tables + three pure functions. No `provenance_generator` import.
137	2. **`run_honest_sweep_r3.py` `build_intro_and_scope_md` (:6398) → `build_framing_md(contract, archetype)`.** Replace the hardcoded `## Introduction and Scope` string with `f"## {archetype.framing_title}\n\n"` (emit nothing when `framing_title == ""`, i.e. memo). Keep the claim-free/citation-free prose class byte-for-byte otherwise.

165	### 3.2 Signal→(tier,kind) model (journal ⇒ PASS is one T1 instance)
166	
167	```python
168	# quality_eligibility.py — replace the journal-only "4.5 second-chance" with:
169	def _positive_signal_tier(row, url) -> tuple[str, str] | None:   # -> (tier, kind) or None
170	    ok, _basis = _has_doi_or_journal_credential(row, url)  # REUSE landed predicate = T1-scholarly
171	    if ok:
172	        return ("T1", "peer_reviewed_journal")             # journal ⇒ PASS is JUST THIS ROW
173	    if _official_gov_host(url):        return ("T1", "government")
174	    if _reputable_newsroom(row, url):  return ("T2", "news")
175	    if _issuer_primary(row, url):      return ("T3", "press_release" if _is_pr(row) else "corporate")
176	    return None
177	
178	# Resolution rule — inserted where the "4.5" second-chance is today (after every FAIL return,
179	# before the UNKNOWN shell/no-metadata returns):
180	#   sig = _positive_signal_tier(row, url)
181	#   if sig:
182	#       tier_id, kind = sig
183	#       if tier_id == "T1":                                      -> PASS  (quality is quality, any contract)
184	#       elif kind in allowed_kinds and kind not in excluded_kinds:-> PASS  (contract explicitly wants this kind)
185	#   # else fall through to the existing UNKNOWN + demote weight (unchanged)
186	```
187	
188	- **T1 = authoritative-universal → PASS unconditionally** (peer-reviewed-journal AND gov both T1). **T2/T3 PASS iff kind ∈ allowed_kinds and not excluded.** Journal⇒PASS is the T1-scholarly row.
189	- **Over-engineering guard (all three):** keep the table to ≤4 predicates over already-fetched fields; **no credibility ontology.** If `_official_gov_host` / `_reputable_newsroom` / `_issuer_primary` are not cheaply available from the existing facet/tier classifier, ship **T1-scholarly + the `kind ∈ allowed` gate seeded by the existing journal helper** for v1 and leave T2/T3 host predicates as a labelled TODO. The *shape* (signal→tier→∈allowed) is the deliverable; adding a kind later must be data, not a branch.
190	- **DOI-alone caveat (Codex/Kimi):** we reuse `_has_doi_or_journal_credential`, which already rejects preprint DOIs (its genre classifier requires article/review + journal source-type; the landed `is_citeable_journal` rejects `_PREPRINT_DOI_MARKERS`). So the DOI path is *not* "DOI alone ⇒ T1"; it is "registered scholarly DOI that also passes the genre predicate." Keep it, but add the property test that a preprint/dataset DOI does **not** PASS (M8).

209	### 3.4 Invariants and how each is coded
210	
211	- **INV-1 never re-admit a strict_verify FAIL.** All Fix-5 code is *eligibility* (which rows may enter the citable menu), always upstream of the frozen verifier. Eligibility neither reads nor writes verify verdicts; a PASS-promoted row's claims still go through the untouched verifier. Enforced by C1's 0-diff check.
212	- **INV-2 never override an EXCLUSION.** **Load-bearing layer = the upstream exclude facet mask** (`op="exclude"`, hard iff NOT_IN term FORCE_HARD), which removes excluded-kind rows from the menu *before* selection — so a T1 `.gov`-hosted **blog** under exclude-blogs never cites even though its tier rule is unconditional T1 PASS. (OPUS correction of Fable/Kimi, who leaned on the tier rule; the T1 clause is unconditional, so the *mask* is the guarantee.) Secondary: `_positive_signal_tier`'s T2/T3 clause checks `kind not in excluded_kinds`.
213	- **INV-3 evidence-POSITIVE only (verdict monotonicity).** The inserted block contains ONLY `return PASS`; every FAIL return is lexically above and untouched; UNKNOWN fall-throughs remain the else-path. Property: `∀ row,contract: new_verdict ∈ {old_verdict} ∪ ({PASS} if old_verdict==UNKNOWN else {})`. The kind-match ordering assigns no weights/verdicts. **Kimi's FAIL-relaxation guard is REJECTED here** (it would violate this).
214	
215	### 3.5 Corpus-adequacy + disclosure fallback (any kind) + acquisition receipt
216	
217	```python
218	# quality_eligibility.py (new, ~15 LOC — NOT a new module)
219	def corpus_kind_adequacy(rows, allowed_kinds, *, min_rows=25) -> tuple[bool, int]:
220	    n = sum(1 for r in rows
221	            if classified_kind(r) in allowed_kinds
222	            and score_source_quality(r, allowed_kinds=allowed_kinds)[0] != FAIL)
223	    return (n >= min_rows, n)
224	```
225	
226	At the sweep call-site the kind-restriction hard mask arms **only** as
227	`term_is_hard AND env_flag(PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_HARD) AND adequate AND acquisition_receipt_matches`,
228	else force degrades to *prefer* + one disclosure line in the machinery appendix:
229	
230	> "Scope note: the prompt restricts sources to {kinds}; only {n} in-scope sources were retrievable, below the {min_rows} adequacy floor — in-scope sources were prioritized rather than exclusively enforced."
231	
232	- **Adequacy counts in-scope-kind rows, not DOI rows** (3/3). Adequacy gates only the eligibility hard mask; retrieval-side go-find (C2) always runs, keeping the count honest.
233	- **Acquisition receipt (Codex, adopted lightweight):** the hard mask also requires proof the corpus was fetched *under this contract's kind lanes* — a `{contract_hash, source_policy_applied}` check against the policy hash. A **frozen/replayed unscoped corpus never arms the hard mask**, it degrades to prefer+disclose. This is the strongest available C2 guarantee against the 997→131 replay; adopt the cheap form (contract-hash match on the retrieval receipt already stamped on `RetrievalPolicy.contract_hash`).
234	- **Flag rename:** `PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_JOURNAL_ONLY` → **`PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_HARD`** (kind-agnostic; reads kinds from the policy). Default **OFF**. No alias (C3).
235

237	
238	- All changes shape the citable **menu** upstream of the frozen verifier; no FAIL row re-admitted (INV-3); UNKNOWN handling adds *evidence*, not leniency; `provenance_generator.py` / strict_verify **0-diff**.
239	- Selection ordering is promote-by-sort only — no weight, no verdict, count-invariant.
240	- **OFF-path byte-identical:** `allowed_kinds`/`excluded_kinds` default empty ⇒ `score_source_quality` byte-identical; `corpus_kind_adequacy` never called without a policy; kind-match sort is a stable no-op when `_allowed` empty; `PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_HARD` default OFF; `RetrievalPolicy.is_empty()` ⇒ caller applies no filter. Champion path (no contract) ⇒ all Fix-5 code no-ops.

Six contract fixtures. **Zero code differences between runs; only the contract is swapped.**

Fix 4: archetype `review`, H1 + "Introduction and Scope", KF after framing; **Methods stays in body** (systematic-review required section); machinery below boundary.

295	## 7. Disagreements & adjudication (code-grounded)
296	
297	1. **Machinery placement: Fable (archetype-invariant, methods always to appendix) vs Kimi+Codex (methods stays in body when a required section).** → **Adopt Kimi/Codex's carve-out.** Code reason: `contract.sections` (`SectionRequirement` list) already exists and already tells us which sections are contract-required; a systematic-review PRISMA Methods is scored content. The guard is 2 lines (`methods_is_machinery = not _contract_requires_section(contract,"methods")`), not a subsystem — cheap and correct. Fable's invariant is right for every archetype *except* the one where Methods is a required deliverable section.
298	2. **Kimi's FAIL-relaxation guard (relax `is_peer_reviewed=False`/low-tier FAIL to UNKNOWN when kind ∈ allowed).** → **REJECT.** Code reason: it violates "evidence-positive only" (INV-3), the one invariant Fable and Codex both hold absolute ("FAIL branches untouched"; Codex: "Explicit quality FAIL precedence remains"). The "high-quality-only + cite-blogs" case Kimi is solving is a genuine user contradiction → `ResearchContract.conflicts`, not a silent relaxation. And `build_quality_eligibility` only arms on `_is_high_quality_request`, so a blogs-wanted-without-quality-clause contract never enters the FAIL gate at all — the case Kimi fears is already handled by the existing `:338` gate.
299	3. **DOI-as-T1: Fable (DOI-or-journal ⇒ T1-scholarly) vs Codex/Kimi (DOI alone insufficient).** → **Keep Fable's reuse of `_has_doi_or_journal_credential`, add Codex/Kimi's guard as a TEST (M8).** Code reason: the landed helper is *not* "DOI alone" — its genre classifier requires article/review + journal source-type and rejects preprint DOIs; `is_citeable_journal` rejects `_PREPRINT_DOI_MARKERS`. So the substance of Codex/Kimi's objection is already met; we lock it with the preprint/shell property test rather than adding a redundant predicate.
300	4. **Codex's 3-way operator split (allowed IN / required REQUIRE / preferred PREFER) vs 2-way (allowed + exclude, force = strength).** → **Defer required-vs-allowed to a labelled TODO; ship 2-way for v1.** Code reason: the landed `RetrievalPolicy` has `allowed_source_kinds` + `excluded_source_kinds` + `predicate_force` only; adding `required_source_kinds`/`preferred_source_kinds` lists is a real data-model change (Codex's own §"minimal policy data model"). Under C3 (prove-not-a-regression, one thing at a time) the hard-vs-soft distinction via `predicate_force` covers the task-72 shape; "REQUIRE = represent-but-don't-exclude" is a genuinely different semantics worth doing *next*, not in the hardening pass. TODO it explicitly so it's data-not-branch later.
301	5. **Codex's acquisition receipt (hard mask requires proof the corpus was fetched under the contract's kind lanes).** → **Adopt, lightweight** (contract-hash match on `RetrievalPolicy.contract_hash`/retrieval receipt). Code reason: it is the strongest guard against the exact 997→131 replay failure — a frozen/replayed corpus can never arm the hard mask. Fable/Kimi's adequacy-count-alone can be fooled by a replayed corpus that *happens* to contain matching rows; the receipt closes that.
302	6. **Heading relabels (Kimi: memo `## Bottom Line`) vs keep `## Key Findings` (Fable).** → **Keep verbatim for v1** (Fable). Chrome churn with no measured payoff; adds anti-hardcode-grep surface for zero IF gain.
303	7. **D8 banner: relocate (Kimi/Codex) vs leave (Fable).** → **Relocate below H1/into appendix in shape-ON mode** (§2.4). Code reason: it directly closes verified-item-1 now, is faithfulness-neutral disclosure chrome (§-1.3), keeps the frozen builder 0-diff, and follows the shipped `compose_report_with_reliability` precedent — no need to wait on Fix 3.
304	8. **The journal-only hard corpus filter (`journal_only_filter.py`).** None of the three saw it (not in the pack), but all three's core thesis condemns it. → **Retire it** (U11). Code reason: a fail-closed corpus filter (`filter_to_citeable` + `assert_no_leak` + `JournalOnlyAbort`) is definitionally the "hard-mask-a-frozen-corpus" pattern C2 forbids; it cannot be made C2-safe by generalizing the kind — the adequacy-gated + acquisition-receipt-gated `PG_SOURCE_RESTRICTION_HARD` *eligibility* path (§3.5) replaces its one legitimate function (honor a hard kind restriction) without starving a frozen corpus.
305	
306	---
307	
308	## 8. Over-engineering watch — what NOT to build (3-way synthesis)
309	
310	- **No generative/LLM report skeleton** (3/3). No LLM intro (Kimi). No audience/tone/length layout logic (Fable/Codex — "tone adaptation belongs upstream in composition," Codex). No per-archetype heading relabels for v1 (Fable).
311	- **No credibility ontology / no LLM inside `score_source_quality`** (3/3). Tier table ≤4 predicates over already-fetched fields; if host predicates aren't cheap, ship T1-scholarly + `kind∈allowed` and TODO the rest.
312	- **Do NOT generalize `_LOW_QUALITY_TIERS` / the quality tier bands.** OPUS code check: `_LOW_QUALITY_TIERS = {T5,T6,T7}` keys on **tier IDs**, not kind words — "industry/news/blog/stub" is only in the human-readable basis string. There is no kind-literal in the mask to generalize away. The contradictory contract is a conflict-ledger case.
313	- **Do NOT rewrite the compiler or the projection** — `predicate_force` from term force is already at `retrieval_projection.py:695`; projection already emits `op="prefer"` (:336).
314	- **Do NOT add `required`/`preferred` source-kind lists this run** (Codex's own model, deferred — §7.4). TODO, not build.
315	- **No "skeleton DSL," no new subsystem, one env flag per fix, default OFF/prior** (3/3). Champion path double-guarded by contract-presence.
316	- **Locate the two unanchored surfaces before writing them:** the S4 in-scope-share audit and (if kept) any residual journal-only wiring in `run_honest_sweep_r3.py`. If inside a frozen file, compute at menu construction. Do not guess.
317	- **The run's job is prove-not-a-regression** — reach ≈ champion IF 0.4587; do not add scope to beat it.
318

content.content:
1	"""Phase C — REAL enforcement of quality + topicality as CITABLE-ELIGIBILITY.
2	
3	This module is the executable core the review (Sol §4 "Make ``high_quality`` real",
4	"Make topicality real"; GATE_REVIEW_VERDICT Phase C item 4) demands. It turns two
5	previously-decorative contract dimensions into deterministic, domain-neutral,
6	POST-FETCH eligibility verdicts over metadata that live retrieval ALREADY fetched:
7	
8	  * ``scope.source_quality`` (e.g. hard "only high-quality / peer-reviewed") ->
9	    a domain-neutral quality profile scored over ``tier`` / ``is_peer_reviewed`` /
10	    ``openalex_venue`` / retraction / degraded-shell flags / OJS-mill host
11	    heuristics. "journal-shaped" != peer-reviewed; a conference proceeding is not a
12	    journal unless the contract allows it. Unknown metadata under a HARD
13	    "only high-quality" fails CLOSED for citable eligibility (disclosed), never
14	    silently relaxed. A SOFT preference reorders only (emits a weight, no drop).
15	
16	  * topicality -> the fetched body (``statement`` + ``direct_quote``) is scored
17	    against the CLEAN objective + owning research thread (NOT the instruction-laden
18	    prompt). Confirmed off-topic (below floor) is quarantined from the citable
19	    menu; uncertain stays and is down-ranked/marked.
20	
21	CRITICAL — this is entirely UPSTREAM of the frozen verifier. It decides which
22	evidence rows are ELIGIBLE to enter the citable menu (``evidence_for_gen``) BEFORE
23	``strict_verify`` ever enumerates the pool. It NEVER changes HOW a claim is
24	verified or cited. ``provenance_generator.py`` / ``strict_verify`` stay 0-diff. A
25	row failing a HARD predicate is REMOVED from the citable menu here but KEPT in the
26	corpus + disclosure (§-1.3 disclose-don't-delete), exactly mirroring the existing
27	scope-hard grounding mask.
28	
29	Everything is pure data: no network, no LLM, no I/O. The whole module no-ops when
30	its inputs carry no hard quality/topicality predicate (byte-identical). It is only
31	reached under ``PG_GATE`` at the citable-menu seam.
32	"""
33	
34	from __future__ import annotations
35	
36	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
37	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
38	from urllib.parse import urlparse
39	
40	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
41	# Verdicts + receipts (spec item 4)
42	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
43	
44	PASS = "pass"
45	FAIL = "fail"
46	UNKNOWN = "unknown"
47	
48	# Stages a receipt can be authored by (routed to the compliance audit).
49	STAGE_QUALITY = "quality_eligibility"
50	STAGE_TOPICALITY = "topicality_eligibility"
51	
52	
53	@dataclass
54	class SourceReceipt:
55	    """One per (source, hard term, stage) eligibility decision.
56	
57	    Consumed by ``contract_compliance.audit_contract``: a hard term is SATISFIED
58	    only if its executable stage ran AND every citable source subject to it carries
59	    a ``pass`` receipt; a single ``fail``/``unknown`` keeps the term unsatisfied
60	    (``unknown`` never fabricated as satisfied — Sol §4 receipt-backed compliance).
61	    """
62	
63	    contract_hash: str
64	    term_id: str
65	    source_id: str            # the row's stable id (source_url)
66	    stage: str                # STAGE_QUALITY | STAGE_TOPICALITY
67	    verdict: str              # PASS | FAIL | UNKNOWN
68	    basis: str = ""           # human-readable rule + evidence that fired
69	
70	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
71	        return {
72	            "contract_hash": self.contract_hash,
73	            "term_id": self.term_id,
74	            "source_id": self.source_id,
75	            "stage": self.stage,
76	            "verdict": self.verdict,
77	            "basis": self.basis,
78	        }
79	
80	
81	@dataclass
82	class EligibilityPlan:
83	    """The post-fetch eligibility outcome over one billed candidate set.
84	
85	    Mirrors ``ScopeEnforcementPlan``: an ``eligibility_excluded_ids`` set the seam
86	    masks out of the citable menu (rows KEPT in corpus + disclosure), a demote
87	    weight map for SOFT preferences (order-not-drop), per-source receipts, and a
88	    disclosure record list.
89	    """
90	
91	    eligibility_excluded_ids: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
92	    url_to_quality_weight: dict[str, float] = field(default_factory=dict)
93	    receipts: list[SourceReceipt] = field(default_factory=list)
94	    excluded_records: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
95	
96	    def is_empty(self) -> bool:
97	        return not (
98	            self.eligibility_excluded_ids
99	            or self.url_to_quality_weight
100	            or self.receipts
101	        )
102	
103	
104	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
105	# Row metadata accessors (domain-neutral; every field is already fetched)
106	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
107	
108	
109	def _row_url(row: Any) -> str:
110	    if isinstance(row, dict):
111	        return str(row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "")
112	    return str(getattr(row, "source_url", "") or getattr(row, "url", "") or "")
113	
114	
115	def _row_get(row: Any, key: str, default: Any = None) -> Any:
116	    if isinstance(row, dict):
117	        return row.get(key, default)
118	    return getattr(row, key, default)
119	
120	
121	def _row_text(row: Any) -> str:
122	    """The fetched-body surface topicality scores against: statement + quote."""
123	    stmt = str(_row_get(row, "statement", "") or "")
124	    quote = str(_row_get(row, "direct_quote", "") or "")
125	    return (stmt + " " + quote).strip()
126	
127	
128	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
129	# QUALITY — a domain-neutral quality profile over already-fetched metadata
130	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
131	
132	# Tier quality bands (tier_classifier.TierLevel): T1 peer-reviewed primary, T2
133	# SR/MA, T3 gov/regulatory, T4 peer-reviewed commentary are the "high-quality"
134	# band; T5 industry-funded, T6 news/blog non-peer-reviewed, T7 abstract/stub are
135	# NOT. This is the primary deterministic quality axis (RECON-2 item 3).
136	_HIGH_QUALITY_TIERS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({"T1", "T2", "T3", "T4"})
137	_LOW_QUALITY_TIERS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({"T5", "T6", "T7"})
138	
139	# OJS-mill / predatory / bare-proceeding host heuristics — domain-neutral URL
140	# substrings that mark a "journal-shaped but not peer-reviewed" venue. Conservative
141	# (a match is a strong NEGATIVE signal, never the sole POSITIVE one). Extensible.
142	_PREDATORY_HOST_PATTERNS: tuple[str, ...] = (
143	    "/ojs/",           # bare Open Journal Systems mill path
144	    "scirp.org",       # Scientific Research Publishing (widely-flagged mill)
145	    "m.scirp.org",
146	    "sciencepublishinggroup.com",
147	    "omicsonline.org",
148	    "omicsgroup.org",
149	    "hindawi-",        # spoofed-hindawi mills (real hindawi.com is NOT matched)
150	    "abacademies.org",  # Allied Business Academies (widely-flagged predatory-adjacent mill)
151	)
152	
153	# Values a hard source_quality term takes that mean "peer-reviewed / high-quality".
154	_HIGH_QUALITY_TOKENS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
155	    "high", "high-quality", "high quality", "peer-reviewed", "peer reviewed",
156	    "peer_reviewed", "reputable", "authoritative", "scholarly",
157	})
158	
159	
160	def _is_high_quality_request(quality_profile: Optional[str]) -> bool:
161	    q = str(quality_profile or "").strip().lower()
162	    return bool(q) and q in _HIGH_QUALITY_TOKENS
163	
164	
165	def _predatory_host(url: str) -> bool:
166	    u = (url or "").lower()
167	    host = ""
168	    try:
169	        host = (urlparse(u).netloc or "").lower()
170	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
171	        host = ""
172	    for pat in _PREDATORY_HOST_PATTERNS:
173	        if pat in u or pat in host:
174	            return True
175	    return False
176	
177	
178	def _has_doi_or_journal_credential(row: Any, url: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
179	    """FIX 5(b): POSITIVE-evidence second-chance signal for an otherwise-UNKNOWN row.
180	
181	    True iff the row carries a DOI (a registered scholarly identifier) OR the deterministic
182	    document-genre classifier resolves it to a peer-reviewed journal/review article. Reuses the
183	    SAME predicate the scope-facet classifier already trusts
184	    (``is_peer_reviewed_journal_article(classify_document_type(...)[0])``) — single source of
185	    truth, no duplicate journal logic. Pure / offline / fail-open: any classifier fault yields
186	    ``(False, ...)`` so a scoring error never RESCUES a row (it can only leave it UNKNOWN).
187	
188	    This is consulted ONLY after every FAIL path has already returned, so it can never
189	    re-admit a retracted / predatory / non-peer-reviewed / low-tier source."""
190	    # 1) DOI: a registered scholarly identifier (row field or a doi.org URL).
191	    try:
192	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.document_type_classifier import _doi_candidate
193	        doi = _doi_candidate(str(_row_get(row, "doi", "") or ""), (url or "").lower())
194	        if (doi or "").strip().lower().startswith("10."):
195	            return True, f"DOI present ({doi})"
196	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open: no rescue on error
197	        pass
198	    # 2) genre: the same peer-reviewed-journal predicate the scope classifier trusts.
199	    try:
200	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.document_type_classifier import (
201	            classify_document_type,
202	            is_peer_reviewed_journal_article,
203	        )
204	        dt, _basis = classify_document_type(
205	            openalex_publication_type=str(_row_get(row, "openalex_publication_type", "") or ""),
206	            openalex_source_type=str(_row_get(row, "openalex_source_type", "") or ""),
207	            openalex_is_peer_reviewed=_row_get(row, "openalex_is_peer_reviewed", None),
208	            source_class=str(_row_get(row, "source_class", "") or ""),
209	            url=url,
210	            title=str(_row_get(row, "title", "") or ""),
211	            doi=str(_row_get(row, "doi", "") or ""),
212	        )
213	        if is_peer_reviewed_journal_article(dt):
214	            return True, f"peer-reviewed journal article ({dt.value})"
215	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open: no rescue on error
216	        pass
217	    return False, ""
218	
219	
220	def score_source_quality(row: Any) -> tuple[str, float, str]:
221	    """Deterministic, domain-neutral quality verdict for one fetched row.
222	
223	    Returns ``(verdict, weight, basis)``:
224	      * ``verdict`` in {PASS, FAIL, UNKNOWN} for a HARD "only high-quality" predicate.
225	        PASS = positively-evidenced high quality; FAIL = positively-evidenced low
226	        quality (retracted / predatory host / low tier / non-peer-reviewed shell);
227	        UNKNOWN = no usable quality metadata (fail-CLOSED at the hard seam).
228	      * ``weight`` in (0, 1] — the SOFT-preference demote (never 0; order-not-drop).
229	      * ``basis`` — the rule + evidence that fired (for the receipt).
230	
231	    "journal-shaped" is NOT peer-reviewed: an ``is_peer_reviewed`` flag that is
232	    explicitly False (fetched, not merely absent) is a FAIL even if the venue looks
233	    journal-like. A retraction is always a FAIL. Absent metadata is UNKNOWN.
234	    """
235	    # 1) retraction — always disqualifying, highest precedence.
236	    if bool(_row_get(row, "is_retracted", False)):
237	        return FAIL, 0.05, "retracted (is_retracted=True)"
238	
239	    url = _row_url(row)
240	    # 2) predatory / OJS-mill host — journal-shaped but not peer-reviewed.
241	    if _predatory_host(url):
242	        return FAIL, 0.1, f"predatory/OJS-mill host heuristic matched ({url})"
243	
244	    tier = str(_row_get(row, "tier", "") or "").strip().upper()
245	    pr = _row_get(row, "is_peer_reviewed", None)  # None = not fetched (absent)
246	
247	    # 3) explicit peer-review evidence.
248	    if pr is True and tier in _HIGH_QUALITY_TIERS:
249	        return PASS, 1.0, f"peer_reviewed=True AND tier={tier} (high-quality band)"
250	    if pr is False:
251	        # journal-SHAPED but explicitly NOT peer-reviewed -> not high quality.
252	        return FAIL, 0.2, f"is_peer_reviewed=False (journal-shaped != peer-reviewed); tier={tier or 'UNKNOWN'}"
253	
254	    # 4) tier-only evidence (peer-review flag absent).
255	    if tier in _HIGH_QUALITY_TIERS:
256	        return PASS, 1.0, f"tier={tier} (high-quality band; peer_reviewed flag absent)"
257	    if tier in _LOW_QUALITY_TIERS:
258	        return FAIL, 0.3, f"tier={tier} (low-quality band: industry/news/blog/stub)"
259	
260	    # 4.5) FIX 5(b): DETERMINISTIC SECOND-CHANCE before the UNKNOWN fail-closed.
261	    # EVERY FAIL path (retraction / predatory host / is_peer_reviewed=False / low tier) has
262	    # already returned above, and so has every positive PASS — so a row reaching here has NO
263	    # negative evidence. If it carries a DOI, or the SAME genre predicate the scope classifier
264	    # already trusts resolves it to a peer-reviewed journal article, that is POSITIVE evidence of
265	    # high quality — PASS rather than fail-close it out of the menu (the 41%-UNKNOWN over-mask).
266	    # EVIDENCE-POSITIVE ONLY: this can NEVER re-admit a FAIL row (they returned above); it only
267	    # rescues UNKNOWN rows that carry an independent positive credential.
268	    _doi_pass, _doi_basis = _has_doi_or_journal_credential(row, url)
269	    if _doi_pass:
270	        return PASS, 1.0, f"deterministic second-chance: {_doi_basis} (evidence-positive)"
271	
272	    # 5) content-shell degradation is a quality-of-content negative but NOT a hard
273	    #    quality FAIL on its own — it is UNKNOWN (fail-closed under hard) with a demote.
274	    if (bool(_row_get(row, "content_starved", False))
275	            or bool(_row_get(row, "landing_page", False))
276	            or bool(_row_get(row, "citation_metadata_shell", False))):
277	        return UNKNOWN, 0.4, "content shell/starved (no quality metadata; fail-closed under hard)"
278	
279	    # 6) no usable quality metadata at all -> UNKNOWN (fail-CLOSED under a hard
280	    #    "only high-quality"; a soft preference keeps it at neutral weight).
281	    return UNKNOWN, 0.6, f"no quality metadata (tier={tier or 'UNKNOWN'}, peer_reviewed absent) — fail-closed under hard"
282	
283	
284	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
285	# The eligibility pass (item 2 + item 3 + item 4 in ONE place)
286	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
287	
288	
289	def build_quality_eligibility(
290	    policy: Any,
291	    evidence_rows: "list[Any] | None",
292	    *,
293	    quality_term_id: str = "scope.source_quality",
294	) -> EligibilityPlan:
295	    """Apply the contract's quality predicate to a billed candidate set.
296	
297	    ``policy`` is a :class:`RetrievalPolicy` (duck-typed: reads ``quality_profile``,
298	    ``predicate_force['quality_profile']``, ``contract_hash``). When no quality
299	    profile is set, returns an EMPTY plan (no-op, byte-identical).
300	
301	    HARD "only high-quality": a row scoring FAIL or UNKNOWN is added to
302	    ``eligibility_excluded_ids`` (fail-closed on UNKNOWN — never silently relaxed)
303	    and carries a FAIL/UNKNOWN receipt; a PASS row carries a pass receipt. SOFT
304	    preference: NO exclusion — only a demote weight in ``url_to_quality_weight`` and
305	    a receipt, so ranking prefers high quality without starving the menu.
306	    """
307	    plan = EligibilityPlan()
308	    quality_profile = getattr(policy, "quality_profile", None)
309	    if not _is_high_quality_request(quality_profile):
310	        return plan  # no hard/soft quality predicate -> byte-identical no-op
311	
312	    force = (getattr(policy, "predicate_force", {}) or {}).get("quality_profile", "soft")
313	    is_hard = str(force).strip().lower() == "hard"
314	    contract_hash = str(getattr(policy, "contract_hash", "") or "")
315	
316	    for row in list(evidence_rows or []):
317	        url = _row_url(row)
318	        if not url:
319	            continue
320	        verdict, weight, basis = score_source_quality(row)
321	        plan.receipts.append(SourceReceipt(
322	            contract_hash=contract_hash,
323	            term_id=quality_term_id,
324	            source_id=url,
325	            stage=STAGE_QUALITY,
326	            verdict=verdict,
327	            basis=basis,
328	        ))
329	        if is_hard:
330	            # HARD: FAIL and UNKNOWN are both out of the citable menu (fail-closed).
331	            if verdict in (FAIL, UNKNOWN):
332	                plan.eligibility_excluded_ids.add(url)
333	                plan.excluded_records.append({
334	                    "source_id": url, "stage": STAGE_QUALITY,
335	                    "verdict": verdict, "basis": basis, "force": "hard",
336	                })
337	        else:
338	            # SOFT: order-not-drop. A FAIL/UNKNOWN demotes; never excluded.
339	            if weight < 1.0:
340	                plan.url_to_quality_weight[url] = weight
341	    return plan
342	
343	
344	def build_topicality_eligibility(
345	    evidence_rows: "list[Any] | None",
346	    *,
347	    objective: str,
348	    thread_queries: "list[str] | None" = None,
349	    floor: float,
350	    scorer: Callable[[str, "list[str] | None", list[dict[str, Any]]], "dict[int, float] | None"],
351	    contract_hash: str = "",
352	    topicality_term_id: str = "topicality",
353	    is_hard: bool = True,
354	    hard_floor: "float | None" = None,
355	) -> EligibilityPlan:
356	    """Post-fetch topical eligibility against the CLEAN objective + owning thread.
357	
358	    Reuses the SAME embedding-cosine scorer live retrieval uses pre-fetch
359	    (``evidence_selector._semantic_relevance_scores``), but as a CONTRACT-AWARE
360	    ELIGIBILITY stage on the FETCHED body (statement + direct_quote), not a prefetch
361	    reorder. Confirmed off-topic (score < ``floor``) is quarantined from the citable
362	    menu (HARD) / down-ranked (SOFT); uncertain (>= floor) stays. A ``None`` score
363	    (embedder unavailable) is UNKNOWN — fail-OPEN for topicality (we never quarantine
364	    a source we could not score; topicality is not a stated user prohibition).
365	
366	    FIX 5(c) — TWO-TIER topicality (only when ``is_hard`` AND ``hard_floor`` is set and
367	    ``0 <= hard_floor < floor``): confirmed junk (score < ``hard_floor``) HARD-quarantines
368	    as before, but the on-topic-ADJACENT band (``hard_floor <= score < floor``) is NOT
369	    dropped — it is SOFT-demoted (a weight, receipt verdict ``FAIL`` but no exclusion), so
370	    the 0.15–0.30 band that was the ACTUAL over-mask stays in the citable menu, ranked down.
371	    ``hard_floor=None`` (default) is byte-identical to the single-floor behavior.
372	
373	    ``objective`` is the clean research objective (contract objective / clean
374	    question), NOT the instruction-laden prompt. ``thread_queries`` are the owning
375	    thread's sub-queries (the per-subquery max clears a focused facet).
376	    """
377	    # Two-tier arms ONLY under a hard predicate with a valid quarantine floor strictly
378	    # below the pass floor; anything else collapses to the single-floor path (byte-identical).
379	    _two_tier = (
380	        is_hard and hard_floor is not None and 0.0 <= float(hard_floor) < float(floor)
381	    )
382	    plan = EligibilityPlan()
383	    rows = list(evidence_rows or [])
384	    if not rows or not (objective or "").strip():
385	        return plan
386	
387	    row_dicts = [{"statement": _row_text(r), "direct_quote": ""} for r in rows]
388	    try:
389	        score_map = scorer(objective, list(thread_queries or []), row_dicts)
390	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a scorer fault is UNKNOWN (fail-open), never a crash
391	        score_map = None
392	    if score_map is None:
393	        # embedder unavailable -> cannot score -> UNKNOWN, fail-OPEN (no quarantine).
394	        for r in rows:
395	            url = _row_url(r)
396	            if url:
397	                plan.receipts.append(SourceReceipt(
398	                    contract_hash=contract_hash, term_id=topicality_term_id,
399	                    source_id=url, stage=STAGE_TOPICALITY, verdict=UNKNOWN,
400	                    basis="semantic scorer unavailable (fail-open, not quarantined)",
401	                ))
402	        return plan
403	
404	    for idx, r in enumerate(rows):
405	        url = _row_url(r)
406	        if not url:
407	            continue
408	        score = float(score_map.get(idx, 0.0))
409	        if score < floor:
410	            verdict = FAIL
411	            # FIX 5(c): under two-tier, only score < hard_floor is CONFIRMED off-topic and
412	            # HARD-quarantined; the hard_floor <= score < floor BAND is on-topic-adjacent and
413	            # SOFT-demoted (kept in the menu, ranked down) even on a hard predicate.
414	            _quarantine = is_hard and (not _two_tier or score < float(hard_floor))
415	            if _two_tier and not _quarantine:
416	                basis = (f"topicality cosine {score:.3f} in soft band "
417	                         f"[{float(hard_floor):.3f}, {floor:.3f}) (on-topic-adjacent; demoted)")
418	            else:
419	                basis = f"topicality cosine {score:.3f} < floor {floor:.3f} (confirmed off-topic)"
420	            if _quarantine:
421	                plan.eligibility_excluded_ids.add(url)
422	                plan.excluded_records.append({
423	                    "source_id": url, "stage": STAGE_TOPICALITY,
424	                    "verdict": verdict, "basis": basis, "force": "hard",
425	                })
426	            else:
427	                plan.url_to_quality_weight[url] = max(0.05, score)
428	        else:
429	            verdict = PASS
430	            basis = f"topicality cosine {score:.3f} >= floor {floor:.3f}"
431	        plan.receipts.append(SourceReceipt(
432	            contract_hash=contract_hash, term_id=topicality_term_id,
433	            source_id=url, stage=STAGE_TOPICALITY, verdict=verdict, basis=basis,
434	        ))
435	    return plan
436

===_is_journal_lead + Fix5d context===
                        # path (a fake llm is injected in the eval harness; the eval
                        # runs OFFLINE on the fixture corpus). One bounded, schema-
                        # constrained call per source over ALL its pending clauses.
                        # Driven on a SEPARATE thread with its own loop (run_one_query
                        # is async / the sweep loop is already running), and the
                        # worker's _RUN_COST_CTX spend delta is written back to the
                        # parent run so PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN stays honest — mirrors the
                        # _planner_llm closure at :9965.
                        import asyncio as _oa
                        import concurrent.futures as _ofut
                        import contextvars as _octx
                        from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import (  # noqa: PLC0415
                            OpenRouterClient as _OJClient,
                            PG_GENERATOR_MODEL as _OJ_MODEL,
                            _RUN_COST_CTX as _OJ_COST,
                        )
                        from src.polaris_graph.planning.eligibility_judge import (  # noqa: PLC0415
                            _build_judge_prompt as _oj_prompt,
                        )

                        _prompt = _oj_prompt(_meta, list(_clauses))
                        _before = _OJ_COST.get()
                        _after_holder: "list[float]" = [_before]

                        async def _oj_run() -> str:
                            _c = _OJClient(model=_OJ_MODEL)
                            try:
                                _resp = await _c.generate(
                                    prompt=_prompt,
                                    system="You are a strict, schema-constrained source-eligibility judge. Output ONLY the JSON object.",
                                    max_tokens=4000,
                                    temperature=0.0,
                                    response_format={"type": "json_object"},
                                    reasoning_max_tokens=2000,
                                )
                                return (_resp.content or "").strip()
                            finally:
                                _after_holder[0] = _OJ_COST.get()
                                if hasattr(_c, "close"):
                                    try:
                                        await _c.close()
                                    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
                                        pass

                        _pctx = _octx.copy_context()

                        def _oj_worker() -> str:
                            return _pctx.run(lambda: _oa.run(_oj_run()))

                        try:
                            with _ofut.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) as _op:
                                return _op.submit(_oj_worker).result()
                        finally:
                            _delta = _after_holder[0] - _before
                            if _delta > 0:
                                _OJ_COST.set(_before + _delta)

                    _o_plan = _build_opaque_elig(
                        _gate_policy, evidence_for_gen, llm=_opaque_llm_judge,
                    )
                    _gate_scope_receipts.extend(r.to_dict() for r in _o_plan.receipts)

1	"""I-deepfix-001 M2 — per-citation document-TYPE WEIGHT-and-DISCLOSE (deterministic, offline).
2	
3	POLARIS has a CREDIBILITY axis (tier T1-T7, ``authority_score``, ``source_class``,
4	``predatory_oa``) but NO orthogonal document-TYPE axis. drb_72 asked for "high-quality,
5	English-language journal articles only," yet the cited corpus is dominated by on-topic
6	*wrong-genre* sources — arxiv preprints, an Amazon book, WEF/IZA/OECD reports, university
7	news blogs, BCG/McKinsey consultancy, and a predatory ``ewadirect`` proceedings venue that
8	*leads* the Corroborated Weighted Findings. There is no per-citation genre label anywhere;
9	the only existing genre mechanism (``journal_only_filter.py``) is a FILTER-AND-DROP +
10	``min_distinct_journals`` COUNT-floor that the operator REVERSED on 2026-06-07 and which
11	must stay dormant.
12	
13	THIS module is the orthogonal WEIGHT axis, NOT a drop:
14	  * ``classify_document_type`` reads the OpenAlex genre signals POLARIS already computes
15	    (``openalex_publication_type`` / ``openalex_source_type`` / ``openalex_is_peer_reviewed``
16	    / ``predatory_oa``) plus a deterministic host/url fallback, and returns a per-citation
17	    ``DocumentType`` label + a short basis string. No LLM, no network, no row mutation.
18	  * ``DEFAULT_DOCUMENT_TYPE_WEIGHTS`` is a multiplicative ``(0, 1]`` SURFACE weight — a
19	    re-rank/disclosure multiplier, never a threshold/floor/cap. Every source still flows
20	    through; a misclassification is a visible label + softened display weight, never a
21	    lost source.
22	  * The whole path is gated by ``document_type_weighting_active`` — a DOUBLE gate
23	    (``PG_DOCUMENT_TYPE_WEIGHT=1`` AND the protocol's ``document_type_preference ==
24	    "journal_article"``). Default-OFF => byte-identical revert (LAW VI).
25	
26	§-1.3 posture: WEIGHT-and-DISCLOSE, not FILTER-and-DROP. This module NEVER calls the
27	reversed ``journal_only`` DROP machinery (``journal_only_active`` / ``is_citeable_journal``
28	exclude branch / ``JournalOnlyLeakError`` / ``assert_no_leak`` / ``prune_contract_plans`` /
29	``min_distinct_journals``). The faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 /
30	span-grounding / provenance) is FROZEN and untouched — the document-type weight is advisory
31	disclosure of the SAME class as the existing ``credibility_weight`` and is read by NO
32	abort/approval/release gate.
33	
34	The journal-POSITIVE test deliberately requires ``source_type == "journal"`` AND
35	peer-reviewed (NOT publication_type alone): the 2025 OPENBIB/QSS literature confirms
36	OpenAlex over-marks ~99% of works as "article," so type alone would mislabel preprints and
37	proceedings as journal articles. This is the same predicate the dormant ``is_citeable_journal``
38	already encodes, reused here as a WEIGHT signal rather than a drop filter.
39	"""
40	from __future__ import annotations
41	
42	import os
43	from enum import Enum
44	from urllib.parse import urlsplit
45	
46	
47	class DocumentType(str, Enum):
48	    """Per-citation document GENRE — orthogonal to the T1-T7 credibility tier."""
49	
50	    JOURNAL_ARTICLE = "JOURNAL_ARTICLE"
51	    REVIEW_ARTICLE = "REVIEW_ARTICLE"
52	    PREPRINT = "PREPRINT"
53	    CONFERENCE_PAPER = "CONFERENCE_PAPER"
54	    WORKING_PAPER = "WORKING_PAPER"
55	    BOOK = "BOOK"
56	    REPORT = "REPORT"
57	    NEWS = "NEWS"
58	    PRESS_RELEASE = "PRESS_RELEASE"
59	    BLOG_COMMENTARY = "BLOG_COMMENTARY"
60	    ENCYCLOPEDIA = "ENCYCLOPEDIA"
61	    DATASET = "DATASET"
62	    UGC = "UGC"
63	    PREDATORY_OA_JOURNAL = "PREDATORY_OA_JOURNAL"
64	    UNKNOWN = "UNKNOWN"
65	
66	
67	# ── Deterministic host/url heuristic fallback sets (LAW VI: module defaults; a protocol /
68	# config may extend them upstream, but these guarantee the offline classifier is never blank).
69	_PREPRINT_HOSTS = frozenset({
70	    "arxiv.org", "ssrn.com", "papers.ssrn.com", "osf.io", "psyarxiv.com",
71	    "researchgate.net", "biorxiv.org", "medrxiv.org",
72	})
73	_REPORT_HOSTS = frozenset({
74	    "weforum.org", "oecd.org", "ilo.org", "imf.org", "worldbank.org",
75	    "mckinsey.com", "bcg.com", "mercatus.org", "brookings.edu", "iza.org",
76	    "ftp.iza.org", "docs.iza.org",
77	})
78	_NEWS_HOSTS = frozenset({
79	    "reuters.com", "bloomberg.com", "ft.com", "nytimes.com", "wsj.com",
80	    "bbc.com", "economist.com",

There is no existing central `row -> source-kind token` classifier. The plan (§3.3/U9) says `_kind_match` uses "same facet classifier" and `classified_kind(r) in _allowed`. Since `classify_document_type` returns a `DocumentType` genre, I'll build a small deterministic `classified_kind(row)` in quality_eligibility.py that maps a row (via the existing `classify_document_type` genre) to a normalized kind token comparable to the free-text `allowed_source_kinds` — reusing the existing classifier (data, not new ontology). And I'll normalize allowed_source_kinds tokens against those.

88	_TRACKING_PREFIXES = ("utm_", "ref", "fbclid", "gclid", "mc_cid", "mc_eid")
89	
90	
91	def canonicalize_url(url: str) -> str:
92	    """Stable canonical key for a source URL.
93	
94	    Lowercases scheme+host, strips ``www.``, drops the fragment and tracking
95	    query params, and normalizes a trailing slash. Used to key the metadata
96	    sidecar so stage merges (base/expansion/deepener/agentic/gap) do not
97	    false-exclude an article discovered via a URL variant (Codex design P2-2).
98	    """
99	    if not url:
100	        return ""
101	    try:
102	        parts = urlsplit(url.strip())
103	    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
104	        return url.strip().lower()
105	    # Normalize scheme to https: http/https of the same resource are the same
106	    # document for citeability, so they must collapse to one canonical key
107	    # (avoids false-excluding a URL variant across retrieval stages).
108	    scheme = "https"
109	    host = (parts.netloc or "").lower()
110	    if host.startswith("www."):
111	        host = host[4:]
112	    path = parts.path or ""
113	    if len(path) > 1 and path.endswith("/"):
114	        path = path.rstrip("/")
115	    # Drop tracking query params; keep meaningful ones (e.g. ?id=, article ids).
116	    kept_q = []
117	    for pair in (parts.query or "").split("&"):
118	        if not pair:
119	            continue
120	        key = pair.split("=", 1)[0].lower()
121	        if any(key == p or key.startswith(p) for p in _TRACKING_PREFIXES):
122	            continue
123	        kept_q.append(pair)
124	    query = "&".join(sorted(kept_q))
125	    return urlunsplit((scheme, host, path, query, ""))
126

179	            key = canonicalize_url(str(raw_key))

183	            for f, v in entry.items():
184	                # Last non-empty / True wins; never overwrite a real value with empty.
185	                if v in ("", False, None) and f in cur and cur[f] not in ("", False, None):
186	                    continue
187	                cur[f] = v
188	    return merged

419	    not import the generator's class.
420	    """
421	    def _set_field(obj: Any, **changes: Any) -> Any:
422	        """Set fields on obj, handling FROZEN dataclasses (Codex diff-gate P2:
423	        ContractSlotPlan is frozen) via dataclasses.replace, plain dataclasses /
424	        objects via setattr, and dicts via item-set. Returns the (possibly new)
425	        object."""
426	        if is_dataclass(obj) and not isinstance(obj, type):
427	            try:
428	                return _dc_replace(obj, **changes)
429	            except (TypeError, ValueError):
430	                # Not all fields are init params; fall back to setattr (non-frozen).
431	                for k, v in changes.items():
432	                    try:
433	                        setattr(obj, k, v)
434	                    except (AttributeError, TypeError):
435	                        pass
436	                return obj
437	        if isinstance(obj, dict):
438	            obj.update(changes)
439	            return obj
440	        for k, v in changes.items():
441	            try:
442	                setattr(obj, k, v)
443	            except (AttributeError, TypeError):
444	                pass
445	        return obj

447	    out: list[Any] = []
448	    for plan in plans:
449	        ev_ids = [e for e in (getattr(plan, "ev_ids", None) or []) if e in kept_entity_ids]
450	        slots = []
451	        for slot in (getattr(plan, "slots", None) or []):
452	            slot_ids = [e for e in (getattr(slot, "entity_ids", None) or [])
453	                        if e in kept_entity_ids]
454	            if slot_ids:
455	                # Rebuild the (possibly frozen) slot with the filtered entity_ids
456	                # so dropped entities cannot survive in a mixed kept+dropped slot.
457	                slots.append(_set_field(slot, entity_ids=slot_ids))
458	        if not slots and not ev_ids:
459	            continue  # whole section pruned away
460	        # frame_rows_by_entity / contract_entities_by_id are dicts: prune keys in
461	        # place (mutating the dict is fine even on a frozen plan).
462	        frb = getattr(plan, "frame_rows_by_entity", None)
463	        if isinstance(frb, dict):
464	            for k in [k for k in frb if k not in kept_entity_ids]:
465	                frb.pop(k, None)
466	        ceb = getattr(plan, "contract_entities_by_id", None)
467	        if isinstance(ceb, dict):

469	                ceb.pop(k, None)
470	        # Recompute focus from surviving slots so no stale non-journal slot label
471	        # appears in prompts/logs/telemetry (Codex design P2-1).
472	        _titles = [getattr(s, "title", "") or getattr(s, "subsection_title", "")
473	                   for s in slots]
474	        _titles = [t for t in _titles if t]
475	        _changes: dict[str, Any] = {"ev_ids": ev_ids, "slots": slots}
476	        if _titles and hasattr(plan, "focus"):
477	            _changes["focus"] = "; ".join(_titles)
478	        out.append(_set_field(plan, **_changes))

503	    predicate in the no-leak assert — its citeability was decided by

516	    (``v30_frame_row``) are exempt.
